User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user (e.g., User, Therapist, Donor, Admin).

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a user's account within the EKA system, linking to various services.

preferences.description: JSON string with user preferences, such as dashboard layout and notification style.

consents.description: JSON string with user consents related to data sharing and AI usage.

startTime.description: The date and time the session started.

endTime.description: The date and time the session ended.

Therapist.title: Therapist
Therapist.description: Represents a therapist in the EKA Account system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the therapist entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the therapist.

lastName.description: The last name of the therapist.

email.description: The email address of the therapist.

specialization.description: The area of specialization of the therapist.

Donation.title: Donation
Donation.description: Represents a donation made within the EKA Account system.

date.description: The date the donation was made.

isAnonymous.description: Indicates if the donation was made anonymously.

Report.title: Report
Report.description: Represents a report generated by a therapist or user.

date.description: The date the report was created.

content.description: The content of the report (e.g., SOAP summary).

aiSummary.description: An AI-generated summary of the report.

name.description: The name of the plan (e.g., Free, Diamond).

Message.title: Message
Message.description: Represents a message exchanged between users.

sentDate.description: The date and time the message was sent.

definition.description: Stores user profiles.  Path-based ownership enforced via security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores account information. Every user and therapist is associated with an account. There will be a 1:1 relationship with user and therapist.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the account.

definition.description: Stores therapy session data.  Path-based ownership under the user. The `therapistId` is denormalized for authorization independence, eliminating the need to fetch therapist details in security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the session.

definition.description: Stores therapist profiles. Path-based ownership enforced via security rules.

params.name: therapistId
params.description: The unique identifier for the therapist.

definition.description: Stores donation records.  This collection is root-level since donations involve two users (donor and receiver). Security rules will ensure that only authorized users can read/write donations where they are either the donor or receiver.

params.name: donationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the donation.

definition.description: Stores reports generated by users or therapists. Path-based ownership under the user. The `therapistId` is denormalized here to avoid `get()` calls in the security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: reportId
params.description: The unique identifier for the report.

params.name: planId
params.description: The unique identifier for the plan.

definition.description: Stores messages exchanged between users. Security rules will ensure that only the sender or receiver can access the message.

params.name: messageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

definition.description: Collection to store admin roles. Existence of a document indicates admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support the EKA Account system, emphasizing security, scalability, and ease of debugging. It leverages denormalization to ensure authorization independence, avoiding complex `get()` calls in security rules and enabling atomic operations. The structure is segregated to maintain a homogeneous security posture within collections and employs consistent access modeling patterns. 

Authorization Independence is achieved by denormalizing authorization data. For instance, the `sessions` collection under `/users/{userId}` contains `therapistId`. Security rules can directly validate if the `therapistId` matches the user attempting to access the document without needing to retrieve the therapist's document.

The structure supports QAPs (Rules are not Filters) by segregating data based on access needs. User-specific data resides under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that listing documents in this collection only returns data accessible to the user. Similarly, public or shared data (if any) would be in a separate collection with appropriate security rules. The Membership Map pattern is implemented through collections like `/roles_admin/{uid}`, allowing for secure `list` operations based on role existence rather than content filtering.
